title: Add a Queue.tryWithLock() method that accepts a timeout.
issue_text:
This PR overloads the `Queue.tryWithLock()` method with a signature that accepts a timeout.

We have an internal plugin that is subject to thundering herd scenarios during high job execution and agent turnover rate. The current use of `Queue.withLock()` in our plugin leads to some long-running requests that lead to client-side timeouts and redundant retries. We would prefer to be able to have a timeout on the lock acquisition to be able to return a 503 response to clients and trigger exponential backoff during these thundering-herd scenarios.

### Testing done

New tests have been added to cover various scenarios of tryWithLock: successful acquisition within the timeout, failure to acquire within the timeout, and acquisition without contention.

### Proposed changelog entries

Since this is just introducing a new utility method for plugin developers, I don't believe this warrants a changelog entry (but I can add one if desired).

### Proposed changelog category

/label skip-changelog
